journal_entry.description: Conversion of preferred stock to common stock
journal_entry.rationale: Under IFRS, conversion of preferred stock to common stock is an equity-to-equity transaction. The carrying amount of preferred stock is removed (debit Preferred Stock to decrease equity) and transferred to common stock (credit Common Stock to increase equity). This is a reclassification within equity with no effect on total equity, assets, or liabilities. The par or stated value of the preferred stock ($100) is transferred at book value to common stock.

output.reason: Converting preferred stock to common stock is an equity-to-equity transaction. On the credit side, we credit Common Stock (equity increase) for $100. The debit side would decrease Preferred Stock (equity decrease). This results in one credit line: equity increase.

credit_classifier.reason: Converting preferred stock to common stock is an equity-to-equity transaction. On the credit side, we credit Common Stock (equity increase) for $100. The debit side would decrease Preferred Stock (equity decrease). This results in one credit line: equity increase.

output.reason: Converting preferred stock to common stock decreases one equity account (preferred stock) and increases another equity account (common stock). The debit side represents the decrease in preferred stock = equity decrease (1 line).

debit_classifier.reason: Converting preferred stock to common stock decreases one equity account (preferred stock) and increases another equity account (common stock). The debit side represents the decrease in preferred stock = equity decrease (1 line).

output.description: Conversion of preferred stock to common stock
output.rationale: Under IFRS, conversion of preferred stock to common stock is an equity-to-equity transaction. The carrying amount of preferred stock is removed (debit Preferred Stock to decrease equity) and transferred to common stock (credit Common Stock to increase equity). This is a reclassification within equity with no effect on total equity, assets, or liabilities. The par or stated value of the preferred stock ($100) is transferred at book value to common stock.

output.reason: Entry correctly records conversion of preferred stock to common stock as an equity-to-equity reclassification. Debits equal credits ($100). Preferred Stock is debited (decreasing equity) and Common Stock is credited (increasing equity), which is correct for this type of transaction. Amount matches transaction text. No tax implications for equity conversions. Entry is complete and directionally correct under IFRS.
